chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI CP. No. D-3215 of 2012 ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Date Order with Signature(s) of Judge(s) For direction For hearing of CMA No.24679 /2018. 06.12.2018 Mr. AhmedAli Ghumro, advocate for the Petitioner, Mr. Shahriyar Mahar, AAG alongwith Mr. Aijaz Ahmed Mahesar, Secretary, Live Stock and Fisheries. **************** Mr. Shahriyar Mahar, learned AAG has filed a concise statement, in compliance of the order dated 01.06.2018 passed by this Court, the relevant portion of the aforesaid report is as under:- “2. In view of observations, this Department took up the matter with the Finance Department on 28-09-2018 (at Annex-IV) with the request for advice for issuance of corrigendum of Audit copy dated 09.10.2017 of the up-graded posts according to cost Centre/DDO code wise. The Finance Department rendered the advice for submitting the case to the Chief Minister on the basis of recommendations of DPC (Annexure-V). SGA&CD Department was also requested for necessary guidance in the matter. The SGA&CD also furnished the advice with the recommendations to submit the case to the Chief Minister Sindh for his approval after rectification of the observation made by the DPC. 3. Accordingly, this Department has moved the summary to the Chief Minister Sindh on 03-12-2018 for decision.” At this stage, Mr. Ahmed Ali Ghumro, learned counsel for the Petitioner has raised his voice of concern that since the Respondent-Department is avoiding to implement the direction issued by this Court vide order dated 01.06.2018 on one pretext or other.

Accordingly, this Department has moved the summary to the Chief Minister Sindh on 03-12-2018 for decision.” At this stage, Mr. Ahmed Ali Ghumro, learned counsel for the Petitioner has raised his voice of concern that since the Respondent-Department is avoiding to implement the direction issued by this Court vide order dated 01.06.2018 on one pretext or other. The learned AAG has refuted the claim of the Petitioner and has submitted that the Respondent–Department is pursuing very hard to implement the directions of this Court and in this regard has placed a copy of Concise Statement dated 04.12.2018 along with annexures. He added that the Competent Authority will pass an appropriate order on the summary moved by the Department on 03.12.2018 for decision. Be that as it may, we are only concerned with the compliance of the order dated 1.6.2018 passed by this Court. The Secretary, Livestock Department, Government of Sindh is directed to pursue the matter with the Competent Authority and obtain orders, on the summary, on the aforesaid issues within a period of 02 weeks’. Let a copy of this order be furnished to the learned AAG for compliance. Adjourned to 14.1.2019. JUDGE JUDGE Nadir/PA
